title: Letter to Sanjay Kumar on CA World 2001
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Post-CA-World-2001 thank-you letter from Peter S. Kastner (Founder and Chief Research Officer, Aberdeen Group) to Sanjay Kumar (CEO, Computer Associates), dated July 19, 2001. Observations include: CA's analyst-relations team (Debra Cattani, Janice Thomas) as industry-leading; CA customer-satisfaction at a nine-year high; the 'CA animosity factor' reduced; press perception lagging reality; dismissive assessment of the 'Ranger situation' (Sam Wyly/Ranger Governance proxy contest); positive review of the 3x6 CA World branding framework.
importance_rationale: Primary-source communication from Aberdeen's Chief Research Officer to the CA CEO immediately before the August-2002 Kumar indictment era; captures Kastner's pre-scandal read of CA customer sentiment and the Ranger proxy contest.
relevance_rationale: Historically important as an on-the-record analyst read of CA weeks before the Wyly Ranger settlement and a year before Kumar's eventual departure.
prescience_rationale: Kastner read the CA-Ranger contest correctly ('non-starter with customers'; the Ranger campaign lost the 2001 proxy fight). Read the CA recovery correctly at a point when press sentiment still lagged.
